topic: GPT-5.5 Pro API Enterprise Guide: $30 per Million Tokens, Highest Accuracy Tier

key_points: Full pricing breakdown: GPT-5.5 standard $5/$30, GPT-5.5 Pro $30/$180 per million input/output tokens
Batch and Flex pricing at 50% discount; Priority at 2.5x standard rate
Long context (>272K tokens) priced at 2x input, 1.5x output for full session
Available in Responses API and Chat Completions API as of April 24, 2026
GPT-5.5 and GPT-5.5 Pro both ship with 1M-token context window
reasoning.effort parameter: low, medium, high, xhigh (default: medium)
GPT-5.5 reaches strong results with fewer reasoning tokens than prior models
GPT-5.5 Pro uses more compute to think harder and provide consistently better answers
Background mode recommended for long-running Pro tasks (several minutes)
GPT-5.5 Pro: 93.6% on GPQA Diamond benchmark
Terminal-Bench 2.0: GPT-5.5 82.7% vs Claude Opus 4.7 69.4%
SWE-Bench: Claude Opus 4.7 leads 64.3% vs GPT-5.5 58.6%
BrowseComp: GPT-5.5 Pro 90.1% vs GPT-5.5 standard 83.4%
Claude Opus 4.7 priced at $5/$25 vs GPT-5.5 Pro $30/$180
GPT-5.5 Pro reserved for Pro, Business, and Enterprise users in ChatGPT
Designed for highest-accuracy outputs and hardest questions
Stronger and more precise tool use on large tool surfaces and multi-step workflows
Multi-turn state handling via previous_response_id in Responses API
40% fewer tokens per task reduces effective cost increase to ~20% for agentic workflows
~20% relative improvement over prior generation on legal research tasks requiring citation of controlling authority
Excels at contract analysis, deal-point extraction, and multi-document review
Reads further into documents to capture survival periods, fraud carve-outs, and jurisdictional conditions
Legal teams use GPT-5.5 Pro via Clio's Vincent agentic legal AI system

stats.fact: GPT-5.5 Pro is priced at $30 per million input tokens and $180 per million output tokens, making it the highest-priced OpenAI API tier as of April 2026
GPT-5.5 Pro achieves 93.6% on GPQA Diamond benchmark, a leading measure of PhD-level scientific reasoning accuracy
GPT-5.5 scores 82.7% on Terminal-Bench 2.0 for agentic tasks, outperforming Claude Opus 4.7 at 69.4%
GPT-5.5 Pro achieves 90.1% on BrowseComp (deep web research), compared to 83.4% for standard GPT-5.5
Batch and Flex processing available at 50% off standard API rates; Priority processing at 2.5x standard rate
OpenAI's finance team used GPT-5.5 (Codex) to review 24,771 K-1 tax forms (71,637 pages), accelerating the task by two weeks
GPT-5.5 requires ~40% fewer reasoning tokens than prior models at the same reasoning effort level, partially offsetting the higher per-token cost for agentic workflows
Prompts exceeding 272K input tokens are priced at 2x input and 1.5x output for the full session
GPT-5.5 Pro is available through the Batch API for multi-turn model interactions and large-scale enterprise processing
Claude Opus 4.7 leads SWE-Bench Pro at 64.3% vs GPT-5.5 at 58.6%, making it still preferable for complex real-world coding tasks

angles: Cost-benefit analysis: When does paying 6x more for GPT-5.5 Pro over standard actually pay off?
Reasoning effort tiers explained: how to control compute spend with low/medium/high/xhigh settings
Enterprise legal ROI: 20% accuracy improvement in contract review tasks translates to real risk reduction
Scientific research breakthrough: GeneBench multi-stage data analysis and genomics workflows
Token efficiency argument: 40% fewer tokens used means effective cost increase is closer to 20% for agentic workflows
Migration guide: moving from GPT-5.4 Pro or GPT-5.5 standard to GPT-5.5 Pro with minimal code changes
Competitor comparison: GPT-5.5 Pro vs Claude Opus 4.7 vs Gemini 3.1 Pro by use case
Batch API strategy for enterprise: combine GPT-5.5 Pro accuracy with 50% batch discount for async workloads
